params.description: Vertical data table appears. Minutes of Practice data: Guitar 27, Piano 14, Violin 41, Drums 33.

beats.text: Here's data about how many minutes students practiced their instruments.

elements.description: Vertical data table showing Minutes of Practice: Guitar 27, Piano 14, Violin 41, Drums 33.

beats.text: Check: are these numbers multiples of 5? Which scale is best for this data?
Which scale is best for this data?
Scale of 2. You saw that 14, 27, 33, 41 aren't multiples of 5 so we need scale of 2 to graph them precisely. Scale of 2 can work when the largest value is 41.

elements.description: Vertical data table showing Minutes of Practice: Guitar 27, Piano 14, Violin 41, Drums 33.

params.description: Vertical bar graph creation tool appears with Scale of 2 active. Minutes of Practice context. Gridlines visible at each tick mark. All bars at 0, ready for student to set heights.

beats.text: Set the bar heights for all 4 categories.

validator.description: Student set all 4 bars to correct heights: Guitar 27, Piano 14, Violin 41, Drums 33

beats.text: You chose the right scale and created the graph. Every bar lands exactly where it should.

elements.description: Vertical data table showing Minutes of Practice: Guitar 27, Piano 14, Violin 41, Drums 33.
Vertical bar graph with all 4 bars correctly placed: Guitar 27, Piano 14, Violin 41, Drums 33. Scale of 2. Gridlines visible.
